example_summary: Total synthesis of (-)-cylindrocyclophane F, a natural product, involving an olefin ring-closing metathesis (RCM) dimerization process to assemble the [7,7]-paracyclophane skeleton, followed by hydrogenation and deprotection.

extracts.transformation_text: Synthesis of (-)-Cylindrocyclophane F from a dimethoxy-bis-alkene precursor via RCM dimerization, hydrogenation, and deprotection.
extracts.catalysts_text: RCM catalyst A (Schrock's catalyst), RCM catalyst B (Grubb's catalyst), RCM catalyst C (Grubb's modified perhydroimidazolidine catalyst), Pd(C)
extracts.conditions_text: 1. RCM catalyst (A: 30 mol%, B: 15 mol%, C: 15 mol%), benzene or DCM, 2-75h, 20-40 °C; 2. H2 / Pd(C); 3. BBr3, DCM
extracts.notes_text: Schrock's catalyst (A) proved to be the most efficient for this transformation. Cross metathesis dimerization process selectively led to the thermodynamically most stable member of a set of structurally related isomers.
